properties.fullDescription: **guide price £450,000 to £475,000**Situated in the idyllic village of Blythburgh lies this very substantial three-bedroom detached house which is beautifully presented throughout. This much-loved family home was built by current owners 40 years ago and boasts an impressive driveway providing off-road parking for numerous vehicles, detached double garage with two sets of 8ft wide double doors which could be extended / developed to potentially run a business from home (subject to planning permission), garage room which is a great multi-purpose room, and stunning side garden which is just exceptional and has a large summerhouse which is fully insulated. The vendor is having oil fired central heating fitted mid August 2026.A summary of the accommodation is as follows: Front porch, entrance hall, sitting room with working open fire, newly fitted kitchen with eco multi-fuel stove, dining room, refitted shower room and separate WC, and garage room to the ground floor. On the first floor are three good size double bedrooms and a refitted shower room.The village of Blythburgh lies on the River Blyth within an Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty approximately four miles of the popular seaside town of Southwold and five miles of the market town of Halesworth. The village is bisected by the A12 commuter trunk road which provides easy access to the inland and coastal delights of this part of Suffolk. There are plenty of local footpaths for walkers to Walberswick on the coast or inland along the River Blyth to Wenhaston and Halesworth. Blythburgh is steeped in history and their Holy Trinity Church is acclaimed as one of Suffolk's finest mediaeval churches. Within the village is a tidal lagoon, Blythburgh Water, which is visited by many mud-loving birds and offers Walberswick Ferry River Trips.Agents Note****Vendor is having oil fired central heating fitted mid August 2026****Front PorchQuarry tiled floor and double-glazed door through to:Entrance HallVertical radiator, granite tiled floor, stairs to the first floor, under stairs recess, door to the kitchen, and opening through to:Sitting Room (4.9m x 3.66m)Double-glazed window to the front aspect, radiator, solid oak flooring, and working open fire with brick surround.Kitchen (3.66m x 2.54m)The stylish refitted kitchen has an extensive range of high gloss eye and base level units including feature curved units and display units, granite work surfaces, butler sink, and water softener to remain. There is an integrated slimline Belling dishwasher, Belling cooker to remain with glass splashback and extractor hood, built-in wine rack, eco multi-fuel stove, tiled floor, ceiling inset spotlights, double-glazed window to the rear aspect, door to the rear lobby, and brick archway through to:Dining Room (3.02m x 2.54m)Double-glazed window to the rear aspect, radiator, parquet flooring, and coved ceiling.Rear LobbyCeiling inset spotlights; doors to the cloakroom, shower room, garage room, and covered lean-to; and access

properties.fullDescription: *Guide £450,000 to £475,000* Beautifully maintained period home full of character and charm Spacious lounge with multi-fuel stove, double-glazed sash windows, and original features Three generous first-floor double-bedrooms plus a charming single/play/music/loft room Private rear garden – Beautifully landscaped and terraced with patios, raised beds, and seating areas. Prime location in Halesworth, just a short walk to the town centre and the train station Attik are delighted to offer this beautifully presented period home set just moments from the centre of Halesworth, a thriving market town with excellent amenities and a strong sense of community. Offering spacious and light-filled accommodation over three floors, the property blends charming original features with thoughtful modern updates. Highlights include a handcrafted kitchen, a cosy lounge with a multi-fuel stove, and four bedrooms including a versatile loft room. Outside, the landscaped rear garden offers multiple seating areas, ornamental planting, and complete privacy - all just a short drive from the stunning Suffolk coast, with Southwold and Walberswick within easy reach. Call the team now to view!The Three-Storey Georgian Home...Tucked away just moments from the charming heart of Halesworth, this beautifully presented period residence offers a wonderful opportunity to enjoy town-centre living with a delightful blend of character, comfort, and space. 57 Quay Street is perfectly positioned - just a five-minute stroll into the bustling high street with its independent shops, cafés, and the renowned New Cut Arts Centre - while also placing you within easy reach of the stunning Suffolk Heritage Coast, with Southwold and Walberswick only a short drive away.Step inside to a welcoming hallway stretching the full depth of the house. The lounge is spacious and light-filled, complete with sash windows (double-glazed throughout), exposed floorboards, a multi-fuel stove, and built-in cabinetry flanking the fireplace. At the heart of the home lies a handcrafted kitchen-dining room, with solid wood units, stylish tiling, a movable island, and modern appliances including a five-burner gas hob and integrated double oven. A glazed door opens onto the rear patio, seamlessly connecting indoor and outdoor living. To the rear, a clever extension houses a boot room, utility space, studio/workshop, and a well-appointed cloakroom, all bathed in natural light and perfect for modern lifestyles.The first floor offers three generously proportioned bedrooms, including a luxurious master suite with built-in wardrobes and a stylish en-suite shower room. A spacious family bathroom features original floorboards and a repurposed vanity unit for a touch of vintage charm. On the top floor, a cosy loft room makes an ideal single bedroom, playroom, creative studio, or a peaceful retreat.The home is set back slightly with both on-road and private driveway parking, comfortably accommodating multiple vehicles. properties.fullDescription: SummaryThis delightful and spacious two-bedroom semi-detached cottage offers a perfect blend of character and modern living. With an open-plan lounge and dining area, a well-equipped kitchen, and a generous rear garden, this home is ideal for first-time buyers or those looking for a convenient town-centre.DescriptionStep inside this charming two-bedroom semi-detached cottage and discover a spacious and welcoming home. The open-plan lounge and dining area create a bright and airy living space, perfect for relaxing or entertaining guests. The well-equipped kitchen offers ample storage and workspace, making it a practical and stylish hub of the home.Upstairs, you'll find two good-sized bedrooms, both filled with natural light and offering plenty of space for storage. The family bathroom is well-appointed, providing a comfortable and modern space.Outside, the property boasts a generous rear garden - a perfect spot for outdoor dining, gardening, or simply unwinding in the fresh air. With its fantastic location close to all local amenities, this cottage is an ideal choice for those seeking a home with character, space, and convenience.LocationSituated in the heart of Halesworth, this property is just a short stroll from the vibrant town centre, offering easy access to local shops, cafés, and amenities. With excellent transport links and the stunning Suffolk countryside on your doorstep, this home provides both convenience and a peaceful retreat.AccommodationGround FloorEntrance HallDoor to Front leading into Home, Access to Dining Room, Access to First Floor landing, Laminate flooring.Lounge 12' 1" x 11' 9" ( 3.68m x 3.58m )Double glazed window to Front aspect, Ceiling Light, Archway leading to Dining Room, Radiator, Laminate wood effect flooring.Dining Room 13' x 8' 1" ( 3.96m x 2.46m )Double glazed window to Rear aspect, Ceiling Light, Radiator, Laminate wood effect flooring.Kitchen 9' 7" x 8' 9" ( 2.92m x 2.67m )Double glazed window to Side and Rear aspect, Door to Side aspect, Ceiling light, Wall and Base units with Work surfaces, Sink and Drainer unit, Oven, Radiator, Laminate wood effect flooring.First FloorLandingAccess to Both Bedrooms and Shower Room.Bedroom 1 10' 7" x 10' 9" ( 3.23m x 3.28m )Double glazed window to Front aspect, Ceiling Light, Radiator, Carpet flooring.Bedroom 2 10' 5" x 9' 3" ( 3.17m x 2.82m )Double glazed window to Rear aspect, Ceiling light, Radiator, Carpet flooring.Shower RoomDouble glazed window to Side aspect, Tiled walls, WC, Wash hand basin, Shower cubicle, Radiator.GardenOutside, the property boasts a generous rear garden - a perfect spot for outdoor dining, gardening, or simply unwinding in the fresh air. PropertyOffered with no onward chain the property is a charming period house with an abundance of character and versatility in the heart of the highly sought-after village of Fressingfield. The house has an attractive symmetrical front facade. The front door opens to an entrance hall with stairs rising directly to the first floor. There are two reception rooms to the front. They both connect to the spacious open plan kitchen/dining/living space that spans the back of the property and is triple aspect. The is the benefit of a separate utility room. There is access from the kitchen to the outbuildings linked by a conservatory/glass house with established vine. There is a ground floor shower room off the conservatory. The first floor landing is split level and gives access to 4 bedrooms and a bathroom.The linked outbuildings are an array of part converted barns and have previously been used as studios and workshops but could be adapted to suit a variety of needs subject to the necessary consents. They offer huge potential. Beyond the outbuildings is the garden which is established with array of mature shrubs and trees and offers a blank page to landscape.To the front of the property is a charming established garden

PROPERTYThe entrance porch opens into a bright and welcoming sitting room featuring a multi-fuel stove, dining area and a useful built-in cupboard housing the water softener. An inner hallway, currently used as a study area, leads off the sitting room and provides access to the first floor.The ground-floor bedroom overlooks the side aspect and benefits from an extensive range of fitted wardrobes and drawer units. To the rear, the well-appointed kitchen enjoys views over the garden and is fitted with high-quality Wren shaker-style wall, base and drawer units, complemented by integrated Bosch appliances including an oven and combination microwave.A door from the kitchen opens onto the garden, while a further door leads to the bathroom, fitted with a bath and shower over, glazed screen, low-level WC, wash hand basin and fitted storage cupboards.The first floor comprises a generous double bedroom with two Velux roof windows, both fitted with electrically operated external anti-heat shutters. A further bedroom also benefits from a Velux window with an internal blind and electric external anti-heat shutter and is served by an en-suite bathroom fitted with a bath, low-level WC and pedestal wash hand basin.OutsideA shin
